title: file-erased event — v1 wire contract
description: FROZEN wire shape for the `storage::FileErased` event. Emitted afterCommit when a File is hard-deleted (row + blob refcount decrement). Distinct from SoftDeletes (which fires no event) and archival (which fires FileArchived). Consumers use this to: remove search-index entries, cascade blob refcount, record erasure evidence for DSAR compliance, revoke lingering signed URLs.

x-versioning.policy: Additive changes stay in v1. Breaking changes create v2 with parallel dispatch through a deprecation cycle.

x-volume: low (~<1 event / tenant / hour typically; spikes during tenant erasure or bulk retention purge)

file_id.description: The erased File's ULID. The row itself is gone at event time; the ULID persists for audit correlation.

sha256.description: Content hash of the erased file. Included for compliance evidence + cross-tenant refcount accounting.

size_bytes_freed.description: Bytes returned to the tenant's storage.bytes.consumed pool. Equals File.size_bytes at deletion time.

erased_by.description: User ULID of the actor. Null for system-initiated erasures (retention purge, tenant cascade).

erasure_reason.description: Why this file was erased. Drives downstream consumer behaviour (e.g. compliance module records dsar_erasure as evidence for the subject's DSAR completion report).

erased_at.description: When the row was hard-deleted. Typically equal to occurred_at.

kind.description: File kind of the erased file. Preserved on the event so consumers don't need to fetch (they can't — the row is gone).

blob_physically_deleted.description: Whether the physical byte was removed. False when reference_count > 1 (another tenant still references the same content-addressable blob); true when this was the last-referrer.
